description: Public Icechunk stores and the addressing forms accepted by GDAL's Icechunk driver (rouault/gdal 'icechunk' branch, PR-as-submitted, commit 8200c07cb3e). The driver delegates array/codec work to GDAL's Zarr driver via a /vsiicechunk/ VSI handler; it Identifies a store by ICECHUNK: prefix, by the 'repo' flatbuffer header, or by a directory containing snapshots/ + transactions/ + (repo OR refs/).

preferred_bare_store_root.why: PR-as-submitted: Identify() detects a directory holding snapshots/ + transactions/ + (repo OR refs/). No braces, no /repo, no /vsiicechunk/. GDAL interrogates the base URI directly. This is the canonical form to lead with.

connection_prefix.why: Explicit driver selection (GDAL_DMD_CONNECTION_PREFIX). Forces the Icechunk driver regardless of Identify heuristics.

repo_file.why: Identify() sniffs the repo flatbuffer header (signature + impl name + spec_version + FILE_TYPE_REPO_INFO). Works on older dev builds too.

vsiicechunk_lowlevel.why: Direct VSI handler. Brace-wrap the path to the 'repo' FILE (not the root). Internally what the driver wraps to as ZARR:/vsiicechunk/{...}. Use for explicit branch/tag and to understand the resolution; quote it (braces/? are shell metacharacters).

single_array_subdataset.why: Address one variable; shown as SUBDATASET_n_NAME in gdalinfo output.

branch_tag.cli: gdal driver icechunk list-branches <store> ; gdal driver icechunk list-tags <store>

credentials_note: All public stores below read anonymously. Pass --config AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST YES (and --config AWS_REGION <region>) for S3, or --config GS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST YES for GCS. The /vsiicechunk/ wrapper carries no credentials; they apply to the inner /vsis3//vsigs/ fetch. Chunk locations resolve across s3://, gs://, gcs://, az://, azure://, http(s)://.
codec_note: Decode is delegated to GDAL's Zarr driver, so codec support == Zarr driver's. blosc/zstd/sharding/crc32c read fine; pcodec is NOT supported -> pcodec stores enumerate metadata but fail on data-chunk reads until GDAL Zarr gains pcodec.

stores.name: dynamical-ecmwf-aifs-single
stores.title: dynamical.org ECMWF AIFS single forecast v0.1.0
stores.status: CONFIRMED full hierarchy (18 subdatasets) + metadata read; blosc/zstd/sharding codecs so DATA reads should work.
stores.bare: gdal mdim info /vsis3/dynamical-ecmwf-aifs-single/ecmwf-aifs-single-forecast/v0.1.0.icechunk --config AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST YES --config AWS_REGION us-west-2
stores.array_shape_observed: [3182 x 61 x 721 x 1440] Float32 (init_time x lead_time x lat x lon); shard chunk_shape [1,61,241,240]; compressor sharding_indexed -> bytes -> blosc(zstd,shuffle), index crc32c

stores.name: earthmover-public-era5
stores.title: Earthmover public ERA5 (ARCO, dual-chunked, pcodec, daily-updating free tier)
stores.status: repo+snapshot resolution CONFIRMED (snapshot GBED7WGX3YHZKVY53DW0). Metadata should enumerate via bare root; DATA reads BLOCKED on pcodec.
stores.catalog_name_NOT_a_bucket: earthmover-public/era5  (Arraylake logical name; do NOT use as an S3 bucket)
stores.bare: gdal mdim info /vsis3/earthmover-icechunk-era5/icechunkV2 --config AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST YES --config AWS_REGION us-east-1
stores.notes: lsm is a static (lat,lon) land-sea mask, fill_value=NaN, chunk [0,0] unmaterialized (Python returns NaN). Missing-chunk->fill is handled correctly in current driver (GetChunkRef returns empty -> VSI Open/Stat return not-found -> Zarr synthesizes fill).

stores.name: icechunk-public-data-glad
stores.title: Icechunk public sample data (GLAD) - from Even's example list (UNVERIFIED here)
stores.status: TODO verify: appeared as a commented example; confirm region + that it is an Icechunk store root.
stores.notes: Was 'gdal mdim info /vsis3/icechunk-public-data/v1/glad --config AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST YES'. Verify region; check root layout (snapshots/ transactions/ repo|refs).

related_non_icechunk_zarr_examples.name: arco-era5-gcs
related_non_icechunk_zarr_examples.title: Google ARCO-ERA5 (plain Zarr v3 on GCS, NOT Icechunk) - useful Zarr-driver comparison
related_non_icechunk_zarr_examples.notes: Plain Zarr store, opened directly by the Zarr driver (no Icechunk). Good baseline to compare Zarr-over-Icechunk vs Zarr-on-object-store.
